evidence: RelationType enum (model/RelationType.java) retains four constants — ADJ_PREDICATE, ADJ_MODIFIER, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF — explicitly documented as 'legacy labels from an earlier exploration subsystem design… no longer set in any grammar config'. They exist only because tests reference them; no production grammar config uses them.

ExplorationResult (model/exploration/ExplorationResult.java) has a 5-arg constructor documented as a 'legacy backward-compat path'; it delegates to the 6-arg constructor with an empty map. Active test code (ExploreResponseAssemblerTest) still calls the 5-arg constructor rather than the canonical 6-arg form.

ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldComparison Javadoc states 'Also reachable at the legacy path /api/semantic-field' but WordSketchApiServer registers no such bare path — only '/api/semantic-field/compare'. This is a stale doc claim about a path that no longer exists.

GrammarConfigLoader.validateNoLegacyKeys actively rejects a 'copulas' key with a clear error message; the comment 'Canonical field name is "pattern"; cql_pattern is no longer supported' appears inline. Both indicate completed, enforced migrations with guards rather than open coexistence.

RelationUtils.RELATION_ALIASES maps old semantic-role names (adj_predicate, subject_of, object_of, adj_modifier) to canonical grammar-config IDs. This is used by active production code (ExplorationHandlers.resolveRelationConfig) to accept old-style HTTP query parameters — intentional backward-compat for external callers, not dead shims.

No mixed JS/TS files, no @Deprecated annotations in production code, no FIXME/TODO referencing 'migrate' or 'remove after', no two concurrent implementations of the same interface. The old query executors and Lucene-only path have been fully replaced by BlackLab.

strengths: Completed migrations are actively enforced: GrammarConfigLoader rejects both 'copulas' and 'cql_pattern' with clear error messages, preventing reversion to old config patterns.
The RelationUtils alias map is intentional, documented backward-compat for HTTP query params — not an accidental shim — and its scope is clearly bounded to a single resolver method.
No two competing implementations of the same interface coexist in production code: BlackLab is the sole query executor, and the old Lucene-only path is gone entirely.
The RelationType constants' legacy status is explicitly documented inline with a precise explanation of why they remain and what has changed, reducing developer confusion.
incomplete_migration.issue_character: The remaining migration residue is contained and self-consistent: dead enum constants retained for test convenience, one stale Javadoc path claim, and a 5-arg convenience constructor that tests haven't yet migrated to the 6-arg form — all localized, none blocking active functionality.
incomplete_migration.score_rationale: The codebase has completed its major migration (old query executor gone, grammar config keys enforced) and the remaining issues are minor: four dead enum constants only referenced by tests (documented but not removed), a stale Javadoc path reference, and a convenience constructor used only in tests. These are cleanup tasks, not active coexistence problems. Against global anchors this sits solidly above 'significant drag' (60) — the migration is functionally complete — but falls short of 'solid with minor rough edges' (80–85) because the dead enum constants are named members of a public API enum and the stale Javadoc makes a false claim about a live route. Score of 76 reflects a well-finished migration with identifiable but low-risk remnants.

issues.summary: Four RelationType enum constants are documented dead legacy values retained only for test compatibility
evidence: RelationType.ADJ_PREDICATE, ADJ_MODIFIER, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF are all marked in their Javadoc as 'legacy labels from an earlier exploration subsystem design… no longer set in any grammar config'.
Production grammar config does not use these constants; they are only referenced by test code (RelationPatternBuilderTest lines 120, 128, 136; RelationUtilsTest lines 61, 65).
Their presence in the public enum risks developers using them in new grammar configs or exploration logic under the false impression they are valid relation types.
issues.suggestion: Update the tests to use SURFACE or DEP (which are the actual types used), then remove ADJ_PREDICATE, ADJ_MODIFIER, SUBJECT_OF, OBJECT_OF from the enum. If the semantic role distinction is needed for future work, introduce a separate SemanticRole enum rather than mixing concerns in RelationType.

issues.summary: handleSemanticFieldComparison Javadoc claims a legacy path '/api/semantic-field' exists but the server never registers it
evidence: ExplorationHandlers.handleSemanticFieldComparison Javadoc (line 131): 'Also reachable at the legacy path /api/semantic-field'.

WordSketchApiServer registers only '/api/semantic-field/compare', '/api/semantic-field/explore', '/api/semantic-field/explore-multi', '/api/semantic-field/examples' — no bare '/api/semantic-field' path exists.
issues.suggestion: Remove the 'Also reachable at the legacy path /api/semantic-field' sentence from the Javadoc; the legacy route was apparently cleaned up from the server but the doc was not updated.

issues.summary: ExplorationResult 5-arg 'legacy backward-compat' constructor is still used by test code instead of the canonical 6-arg form
evidence: ExplorationResult.java lines 40–43: a 5-arg constructor labelled in its Javadoc comment as 'legacy backward-compat path', delegating to the 6-arg constructor with Map.of().
ExploreResponseAssemblerTest.java lines 25 and 34 call this 5-arg constructor — active test code has not been updated to the 6-arg canonical form.
issues.suggestion: Update ExploreResponseAssemblerTest to call the 6-arg constructor directly (passing Map.of() explicitly), then remove the 5-arg constructor from ExplorationResult.

root_causes: The migration from the older exploration subsystem was completed functionally but not cleaned up: dead enum constants, a removed route whose Javadoc was not updated, and a convenience constructor added for compat were left in place rather than removed once no longer needed.
Tests were not updated in step with production code changes, preserving references to deprecated paths (5-arg constructor, legacy RelationType constants) that would otherwise have been removed.
likely_symptoms: dead_legacy_relation_type_constants — localized to the enum and tests, not a systemic design problem
